stocks.name: International Business Machines Corporation
stocks.explanation: IBM cratered 26%, its worst day on record, after issuing a preliminary Q2 earnings warning with revenue of $17.2B missing the $17.86B consensus. CEO Arvind Krishna blamed weakness in software and infrastructure as clients shifted spending toward AI servers and memory chips.

stocks.name: Lucid Group, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Lucid bounced 16% on a relief rally after the EV maker forcefully denied bankruptcy reports that had hammered shares earlier this week. Cost-cutting initiatives and renewed optimism around its Cosmos SUV push also supported the rebound.

stocks.name: SK hynix Inc.
stocks.explanation: SK hynix gave back most of Monday's historic 27% rally as profit-taking hit the recently U.S.-listed memory chipmaker. A South Korean brokerage report suggesting current-quarter operating profit may miss estimates added to selling pressure.

stocks.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Applied Optoelectronics reversed sharply, falling 11% after the prior session's 12% surge. The optical networking component maker saw investors locking in profits amid uncertainty about the sustainability of AI data center capital spending.

stocks.name: Tower Semiconductor Ltd.
stocks.explanation: Tower Semiconductor gained 10% extending its semiconductor rally on ASML's raised full-year guidance. The specialty foundry benefits from the broader chip capex upcycle driven by AI demand. The stock has risen over 400% in the past year.

stocks.name: Camtek Ltd.
stocks.explanation: Camtek rose 9% as the semiconductor inspection equipment maker rode ASML's strong Q2 earnings higher. Its metrology systems are critical to AI chip packaging processes, positioning it as a direct beneficiary of accelerating fab investment.

stocks.name: Strategy Inc. (MicroStrategy)
stocks.explanation: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) climbed 7% continuing its recovery from late-June lows. The rally was supported by its new Digital Credit Capital Framework authorizing up to $1B in stock repurchases and Bitcoin holding near $64,600.

stocks.name: Alibaba Group Holding Limited
stocks.explanation: Alibaba jumped 6% after China's Cyberspace Administration approved Apple Intelligence powered by Alibaba's Qwen AI model across iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS, clearing a major regulatory hurdle for Apple's AI features in mainland China.

stocks.name: Atlassian Corporation
stocks.explanation: Atlassian climbed 6% extending a multi-week rally after Gartner named it a Leader in its first Magic Quadrant for Developer Productivity Insight Platforms. A strategic Atlassian Ventures investment in AI-focused Rocketlane also boosted sentiment.

stocks.name: Cisco Systems, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Cisco fell 6% in sympathy with IBM's enterprise software selloff as investors questioned whether IT budget shifts toward AI hardware are coming at the expense of traditional networking and infrastructure spending.

stocks.name: Marvell Technology, Inc.
stocks.explanation: Marvell continued its slide, down 5% extending a week of losses totaling over 13%. The custom silicon chipmaker is facing valuation compression amid concerns over the sustainability of enterprise AI spending on networking hardware.

stocks.name: Alphabet Inc.
stocks.explanation: Alphabet rose 4% as cooler inflation data lifted big-cap tech and investors rotated into AI platform leaders. Google's recent Gemini Enterprise launch at Cloud Next '26 positioned it as a direct competitor in the enterprise AI market.

stocks.name: Qualcomm Incorporated
stocks.explanation: Qualcomm dropped 4% as the mobile chipmaker faced headwinds from the broader semiconductor rotation. While AI chip demand remains strong, Qualcomm's handset and automotive exposure left it trailing pure-play AI semiconductor names.

stocks.name: ASML Holding N.V.
stocks.explanation: ASML rose 2.4% after reporting Q2 revenue of 9.33B euros, beating estimates by 400M euros. The company raised its full-year sales forecast for the second time in 2026 to 43-45B euros, citing relentless AI chip demand.

stocks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
stocks.explanation: Nvidia gained 3% on multiple tailwinds: ASML's earnings confirmed sustained AI chip demand, H200 shipments to China have restarted, and cooler-than-expected PPI data supported broader tech sentiment.
